notes: First bake of the base — confirm the cornstarch keeps it soft at 2 scoops protein, and that 163 kcal / 14.3 g protein eats like a real crumbl base. If texture holds, bump dough protein 2 -> 2.5 scoops to crack <10 kcal/g.

Oat-base branch of the red velvet bake-off - rehabbed for floppiness: less pumpkin, double the oat flour, an egg white, no cornstarch, baked 325F/15-18 min. Bake vs the Crumbl-base version, then promote the winner.
Good taste. Texture came out soft, not very dense as the recipe intends — acceptable, not much to be done about it.
Decent. Filling really wants a Ninja Creami churn for texture.

Test the <10 kcal/g-protein claim IRL — 3 scoops whey in 5 cookies is aggressive protein-loading; watch for dry/rubbery 'pancake' texture and whether the 1-cup-yogurt frosting reads as frosting or just wet.
found on IG, untried - lean yogurt-based cookie
glaze too weak/thin, needs work
cookie itself is great
Crumbl-base branch of the red velvet bake-off - red velvet on the proven family chassis (cocoa + vinegar tang, cream-cheese frosting). Bake vs the oat-base version, then promote the winner.

Made and fully tasted — excellent, came out amazing. ~217 cal / 19 g protein per slice (11.5 cal/g).
Test bake: lower-butter oat choc-chip version. Check chew/spread and whether 8 g butter + the milk splash keeps it from drying out; adjust milk to hydrate the oats.

First coconut build: crustless, 45g filling + 20g topping, toasted shredded only. Bake and taste; if coconut reads too subtle add 1 tsp coconut extract (0 cal) rather than more shredded (which only adds fat). Fat is ~9.6 g/slice.

First brulee build: vanilla base + 2 tsp vanilla + ~20 g brown-sugar brulee shell (broiler or dry-pan shards). Bake, chill, brulee at serving time. Check the shell sets crisp and the cake does not warm under the broiler; tune sugar amount for shell thickness.
Made — excellent. The fudge-max original; both the smooth and the chunky finish come from this one base.
Made — good. Egg+white+brown-sugar glossy beat (no aeration) + dropped cornstarch lands the fudgy/chewy balance.
Bake-and-taste: does beating egg+white+brown sugar to glossy (no aeration) + dropped cornstarch land the fudgy/chewy balance? Ratio 12.5.
Made — good. Chew from oil + the extra fold strokes; leanest of the three.
Bake-and-taste: oil + most brown sugar + 10-15 extra fold strokes for gluten — chewy without drying? Leanest at 12.4 cal/g protein.
Good. Next time use way more milk in BOTH the crust and filling or it doesn't spread right; press in a loaf pan.
Pretty good.
Really good - near-favorite. Microwave 6-7s straight from the freezer.
